# UC-10.13.3 · Impossible Travel Detection (CIM)

## Description

Two successful auths from distant `src` locations within a window that precludes travel — vendor-agnostic when geo is mapped to CIM.

## Value

Two successful auths from distant `src` locations within a window that precludes travel — vendor-agnostic when geo is mapped to CIM.

## Implementation

Enrich with `src_lat`/`src_lon` for haversine distance when available; whitelist VPN egress ASNs; tune time window per org.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: IdP + iplocation enrichment in TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: CIM Authentication with `src` populated.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enrich with `src_lat`/`src_lon` for haversine distance when available; whitelist VPN egress ASNs; tune time window per org.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=authentication action=success user=*
| iplocation src
| streamstats window=2 global=f current(Country) as cur_c prev(Country) as prev_c current(_time) as ts prev(_time) as pts by user
| where isnotnull(prev_c) AND cur_c!=prev_c AND (ts-pts)<3600
| table _time, user, src, cur_c, prev_c, ts, pts
```

Understanding this SPL

**Impossible Travel Detection (CIM)** — Two successful auths from distant `src` locations within a window that precludes travel — vendor-agnostic when geo is mapped to CIM.

Documented **Data sources**: CIM Authentication with `src` populated. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): IdP + iplocation enrichment in TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Pipeline stage (see **Impossible Travel Detection (CIM)**): iplocation src
• `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(prev_c) AND cur_c!=prev_c AND (ts-pts)<3600` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Impossible Travel Detection (CIM)**): table _time, user, src, cur_c, prev_c, ts, pts

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` dc(Authentication.src) as srcs from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where Authentication.action="success" by Authentication.user, _time span=15m
| where srcs>1
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Impossible Travel Detection (CIM)** — Two successful auths from distant `src` locations within a window that precludes travel — vendor-agnostic when geo is mapped to CIM.

Documented **Data sources**: CIM Authentication with `src` populated. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): IdP + iplocation enrichment in TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Filters the current rows with `where srcs>1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Map (user path), Table (violations).

## SPL

```spl
index=security tag=authentication action=success user=*
| iplocation src
| streamstats window=2 global=f current(Country) as cur_c prev(Country) as prev_c current(_time) as ts prev(_time) as pts by user
| where isnotnull(prev_c) AND cur_c!=prev_c AND (ts-pts)<3600
| table _time, user, src, cur_c, prev_c, ts, pts
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` dc(Authentication.src) as srcs from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where Authentication.action="success" by Authentication.user, _time span=15m
| where srcs>1
```
